tldr: Headlines overwhelmingly lead with Mexico's historic milestone as the first team to reach the 2026 World Cup knockout stage, while South Korean outlets uniquely reframe the same event as a national defeat colored by a recurring cultural 'Match 2 jinx' and resilience messaging from the national coach. The pan-African outlet alone centers fan celebrations over the scoreline.

frames.sub_frame: S. Korea jinx vs. host-nation celebration

evidence.quote: Celebrations as Mexico becomes first nation
S. Korea coach implores players to hold heads high
S. Korea in good position to reach knockouts

bottom_line: Headline coverage presents Mexico's knockout qualification as an uncontested historic milestone; only South Korean outlets reframe the same event as national setback, invoking a recurring cultural jinx and publishing a resilience counter-narrative.
